anchor:
🚀 We’re scaling fast and need a **Senior Cross‑Platform Mobile Engineer (Flutter, Dart)** to lead our mobile strategy for a next‑gen fintech app.

- 4+ years building production‑grade apps, primarily with **Flutter 3+** and **Dart**  
- Deep expertise in **state‑management** (Riverpod, Bloc, Provider) and UI/UX design principles for Android, iOS, and web‑responsive layouts  
- Proven record integrating **GraphQL**, RESTful APIs, and real‑time sockets (WebSocket, Firebase Realtime)  
- Experience setting up CI/CD pipelines (GitHub Actions, Bitrise, Fastlane) and automated test suites (Flutter Test, Integration Test, e2e with Appium or Detox)  
- Comfortable with feature toggles, OTA updates, and push‑notification workflows (Firebase Cloud Messaging, PushKit)  
- A collaborative player who thrives in Agile/Scrum, mentors junior devs, and works cross‑functionally with Product, UX, QA, and DevOps to deliver pixel‑perfect releases  
- Passion for performance optimization (frame‑rate, memory usage, battery life) and code quality (linting, static analysis)  
- Remote‑first with a preference for US time‑zone flexibility, but ideally based in the San Francisco Bay Area or willing to relocate by Q3 2026  
- Full‑time, not C2C; start ASAP (within 2‑3 weeks of an offer)  
- Comfortable owning a full mobile feature lifecycle: ideation, implementation, QA, launch, and post‑launch monitoring  

If you’re a Flutter veteran who loves building polished, scalable mobile experiences—and want to shape the future of fintech—let’s chat!

positive.summary: Senior Flutter mobile engineer with 7+ years of experience building production-grade fintech applications, specializing in cross‑platform state management, backend integration, CI/CD, automated testing, and performance optimization.

work_experience.description: Built cross‑platform music streaming client with Flutter 2, later migrating to Flutter 3+. Developed real‑time audio streaming via WebSocket, integrated RESTful APIs for playlist management, and optimized UI for high frame‑rate and low battery consumption. Established CI/CD pipelines with Bitrise, Fastlane, and GitHub Actions, and collaborated closely with product, UX, QA, and DevOps to deliver user‑centric releases.

Led Flutter-based payment and wallet services for Careem’s fintech suite, integrating GraphQL, REST, WebSocket, and Firebase Realtime to provide seamless mobile banking experiences. Designed and implemented CI/CD pipelines using GitHub Actions, Bitrise, and Fastlane, and established a comprehensive automated testing strategy with Flutter Test, Integration Test, and Detox e2e. Managed feature toggles via Firebase Remote Config, OTA updates through Firebase App Distribution, and push notifications using Firebase Cloud Messaging and PushKit. Mentored junior developers in state‑management patterns (Riverpod, Bloc, Provider) and championed UI/UX consistency across Android, iOS, and web‑responsive layouts.

projects.name: FinPay Mobile
projects.description: A cross‑platform fintech app featuring payment processing, real‑time transaction updates, and personalized budgeting tools.

projects.name: PushNotify
projects.description: Reusable Flutter library to simplify push notification integration across Android, iOS, and web, supporting Firebase Cloud Messaging and PushKit.

negative.summary: Principal Site Reliability Engineer with 12 years of experience architecting high‑availability multi‑region cloud platforms, mastering AWS, Kubernetes, and Terraform.

work_experience.description: Automated deployment pipelines for Anghami’s streaming services, leveraging Docker, Helm, and Terraform. Managed CI/CD workflows in Jenkins and GitLab CI, and monitored system health with Prometheus and Grafana.

Built scalable microservice infrastructure for Noon’s e‑commerce platform, implementing multi‑region deployments and automated scaling. Led Kubernetes cluster management and introduced GitOps with ArgoCD, integrating CI/CD pipelines via Jenkins X and GitLab CI. Enhanced observability with Prometheus, Grafana, and Datadog.

Architected and led the evolution of Careem’s global cloud platform, delivering fault‑tolerant services across 6 availability zones with blue‑green and canary strategies. Implemented GitOps with ArgoCD, automated elasticity via AWS Auto Scaling and custom Kubernetes operators, and championed zero‑trust security with IAM, KMS, Secrets Manager, and Network Policies.

projects.name: Global AWS Multi‑Region Platform
projects.description: Designed a cross‑region infrastructure using Terraform and Kubernetes Operators, enabling blue‑green and canary deployments with AWS Auto Scaling.

projects.name: Chaos Engineering Framework
projects.description: Implemented automated chaos experiments with LitmusChaos and Gremlin, integrated into the CI pipeline to validate resilience of microservices.

projects.name: Observability Stack Consolidation
projects.description: Unified metrics and logs across services using Prometheus, Loki, Tempo, Datadog, and New Relic, providing end‑to‑end visibility with Grafana dashboards.

Hey team!  
We’re scaling fast and looking for a mid‑level front‑end engineer (3–5 yrs) who loves building accessible, high‑performance UIs with React and Next.js. #TypeScript, Storybook, and a knack for performance tuning are musts, and a hit with design‑collaboration is a plus.  
Remote is cool—open to U.S. time zones or an in‑office option in Boston. Full‑time position with a solid salary, benefits, and the chance to help shape a new component library. If you know someone who fits, hit me up!

anchor:
Hey talent! 🙌  
Looking for a **Senior Frontend Engineer** (Lead, 7+ yrs exp.) specialized in **React + Next.js + Tailwind** who can architect and ship a company‑wide **design system** and champion **accessibility & performance** at scale.

**Key ingredients:**

- Full‑stack JavaScript/TypeScript mastery with React hooks; solid grasp of **Next.js** image/SSG/ISR, dynamic routes, and API routes.  
- Experience building reusable component libraries in Tailwind + **Styled‐Components** or **Emotion**; comfortable with **Storybook** for component isolation and documentation.  
- Proven track record writing **test‑driven, unit‑&‑integration‑tests** in Jest + React Testing Library; CI/CD automation (GitHub Actions, Vercel, Netlify).  
- Deep familiarity with **headless CMS** (Contentful, Strapi) integration, and **GraphQL** or **REST** API consumption.  
- Advocate for **accessibility (WCAG 2.1 AA)**, test with aXe, and browser‑compatibility across Chrome, Safari, Firefox, Edge.  
- Lead or mentor a small squad of junior/mid devs; write design documents, conduct code reviews, and drive “best practices” chats.  
- Prior experience evangelizing **performance audits** (Lighthouse), **Bundle size** optimization, and **critical‑path CSS** generation.  

**What we’re offering:**

- Base salary **$155k–$190k** (with significant bonus potential).  
- Fully remote (HQ in San Francisco/NYC) or on‑site options in **San Jose, Seattle, CA, NY**.  
- **Hybrid** work flexibility (3‑days in office).  
- Stock options + 15‑day PTO + health/vision/dental.  

**Who we’re looking for:**

- Demonstrated leadership (team or project), excellent communication, and a passion for modern frontend tooling.  
- Comfortable handling **cross‑functional** collaboration—UX, product, backend.  
- A fresh, innovative mindset: can tackle bleeding‑edge features (streaming data, real‑time collab, or AI snippets).  

Drop me a DM if you’re hiring in this space—or let me know if you have a fit! Let’s build products that feel great to use. 🚀

positive.summary: Lead Frontend Engineer with 11 years of experience building scalable design systems, performance‑optimized web applications, and mentoring teams across MENA tech companies.

work_experience.description: Developed and maintained Swvl’s booking app, building a reusable component library, optimizing bundle size, and leading a small frontend team.
Architected Anghami’s global design system, integrated headless CMS, led accessibility audits, and performed performance optimizations.
Driving front‑end engineering excellence, championing CI/CD pipelines, mentoring a squad, and building AI‑powered features.

projects.name: DesignSystem Pro
projects.description: A comprehensive design system built with React, Next.js, Tailwind, Styled‑Components, and Storybook.

projects.name: PerformanceHub
projects.description: Performance monitoring dashboard integrating Lighthouse, bundle analyzer, and critical‑path CSS extraction.

projects.name: ContentSync
projects.description: Headless CMS integration with Contentful and dynamic route generation for SEO‑friendly pages.

work_experience.description: Built internal data processing pipelines and REST APIs. Leveraged Redis for caching and improved response time by 30%. Contributed to code reviews and automated test suites.

Developed and maintained microservices using Django and Django REST framework. Implemented gRPC endpoints for real‑time data exchange and optimized PostgreSQL queries. Dockerized services for CI/CD pipelines and authored comprehensive unit and integration tests with Pytest.

projects.description: Real-time inventory tracking microservice built with Django, DRF, PostgreSQL, and Redis. Exposed gRPC endpoints for high‑throughput updates and integrated Docker for scalable deployment.

projects.name: OrderProcessingFlow
projects.description: Designed and implemented an order processing workflow using Django, Celery, and Redis queues. Added automated tests and documented API contracts.

Participated in on‑call incident response, managed alerts via PagerDuty and Microsoft Teams, and enforced security policies with Azure Security Center.

projects.name: AzureObservabilityDashboards
projects.description: Designed Grafana dashboards using Azure Monitor and Log Analytics to provide real‑time insights into application performance and reliability.

projects.name: AKSAutoScaler
projects.description: Implemented auto‑scaling for AKS clusters with Terraform, integrating Azure Monitor for metrics and alerts.

projects.name: OpenTelemetryPythonExporter
projects.description: Created a lightweight Python exporter for OpenTelemetry to capture custom metrics from Azure Functions.

anchor:
We’re expanding our cloud security team and looking for a **Cloud‑Native IAM Engineer** (mid‑level, 2‑4 yrs experience) to build Zero‑Trust access controls across AWS, Azure, and GCP.

* **Key skills**:  
  * Hands‑on experience with Terraform, Ansible, and Kubernetes RBAC.  
  * Demonstrated work with identity federation (OIDC, SAML) and MFA strategy.  
  * Strong grasp of IAM best practices in multi‑cloud environments.  

* **What we need**:  
  * Ability to design and deploy fine‑grained IAM policies that enforce least‑privilege at scale.  
  * Proven record of integrating IAM with CI/CD pipelines (GitLab, Jenkins).  
  * Experience auditing and remediating IAM misconfigurations using tools like Prowler or Cloud Custodian.  

* **Where we’ll work**:  
  * Remote‑first, with occasional visits to our Seattle office for team syncs.  

* **Why jump on board**:  
  * Play a pivotal role in securing our SaaS offering during a multi‑year product rollout.  
  * Direct impact on customer trust and regulatory compliance.  
  * Fast‑track growth path: lead a small security operations team within the next 18 months.  

If you’re a masking‑savvy IAM ninja who can take direction quickly and own critical security initiatives, let’s chat!

positive.summary: Cloud‑Native IAM Engineer with 3 years of experience building Zero‑Trust access controls across AWS, Azure, and GCP.

work_experience.description: Automated IAM policy management and enforced MFA across multi‑cloud environments. Implemented SAML and OIDC federated login for internal services and used Terraform to provision IAM resources.

Designed and implemented Zero‑Trust IAM solutions across AWS, Azure, and GCP using Terraform, Ansible, and Kubernetes RBAC. Integrated IAM with GitLab CI/CD pipelines and automated audit remediation with Prowler and Cloud Custodian.

projects.name: ZeroTrust IAM Platform
projects.description: Built a platform enforcing least‑privilege IAM policies across AWS, Azure, and GCP, integrating Terraform, Ansible, and Kubernetes RBAC with automated audit remediation.

projects.name: Identity Federation Integration
projects.description: Implemented SAML and OIDC federation for 200+ services, establishing MFA strategy and role-based access control.

projects.name: CI/CD IAM Automation
projects.description: Automated IAM pipeline integration with GitLab CI, provisioning IAM resources via Terraform and Ansible.

certifications: Microsoft Certified: Azure Security Engineer Associate
Google Cloud Professional Cloud Security Engineer

negative.summary: Mid-level Frontend Engineer with 4.5 years of professional experience in React and Next.js, specializing in component libraries, SSR, legacy browser support, and accessibility.

work_experience.description: Built patient booking UI with React 16, integrated Redux Toolkit, created reusable components with Emotion and SCSS modules, ensured IE11 compatibility via polyfills, wrote unit tests with Jest and RTL, and documented components in Storybook. Deployed to Netlify using GitHub Actions.

Led the design and implementation of Careem’s company-wide component library using React 17, Next.js 12, and Emotion. Implemented SSR with getServerSideProps, added IE11 polyfills, wrote comprehensive accessibility layers (WCAG 2.0 AA), and achieved 80% code quality with fast debugging. Deployed via Netlify with GitHub Actions, Dockerised dev environments, and added Playwright for E2E testing.

projects.name: Next.js Component Library
projects.description: Designed a reusable component library with Emotion, SCSS modules, and Storybook, supporting SSR and IE11 fallback, deployed on Netlify.

projects.name: E2E Testing Suite
projects.description: Developed Playwright-based E2E tests for a multi‑tenant web app, covering accessibility and performance metrics.

projects.name: Legacy Browser Polyfill Integration
projects.description: Implemented polyfills for IE11 in a complex Next.js application, ensuring full functionality across legacy browsers.
